title: Forest Green Applique Tote
description: A sturdy, fully lined tote bag adorned with whimsical embroidered applique leaf motifs. Features durable wide straps for comfortable shoulder carry.
backstory: The body of this bag was cut from a heavy forest-green canvas army surplus duffel, and the leaves were appliqued using floral-patterned scrap fabrics collected from local upholstery workshops.

title: Retro Orange Sun Hat
description: A soft, reversible bucket-style sun hat in a vibrant retro orange check pattern on one side, and warm cream canvas on the other.
backstory: Crafted from a pair of deadstock 1970s gingham trousers that had damaged cuffs. We saved the top sections to form the outer shell of this playful piece.

spec.care: Spot clean or hand wash only

title: Ocean Blue Denim Waistcoat
description: An open waistcoat constructed from varying shades of light and dark preloved denim, with cross-hatch hand embroidery adding decorative tactile details.
backstory: Created from three pairs of discarded jeans that were too worn out at the knees to salvage as trousers. We stitched the cleanest panels together in a clean mosaic.

title: Cream Crochet Trim Pouch
description: A delicate zippered pouch with vintage crochet trims and a hand-stamped label, perfect for holding small daily essentials or toiletries.
backstory: Features laces rescued from an old tablecloth and lined with soft cream brushed cotton from a vintage bedsheet.

spec.materials: Cotton shell, Metal zipper, Lace trim

title: Patchwork Denim Messenger Bag
description: A rugged messenger bag with an adjustable crossbody strap, custom internal pockets, and a vintage button closure.
backstory: Constructed from repurposed denim work jackets and vintage plaid flannel shirts, giving it a strong structure and cozy lining.

title: Red Vest
description: A vibrant upcycled red vest, handcrafted with a relaxed fit and retro details. Perfect for layering.
backstory: Stitched from repurposed vintage tablecloth linen and deadstock red corduroy fabric in our London workshop.
